Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 楚王 - Refers to a king of the state of Chu during the Warring States period.
  • 云梦 - A region in ancient Chu, known for its marshes and forests, and often used in classical texts as a setting for hunting.
  • 安陵君 - A title given to a noble or court official, indicating a position of honor and trust.
  • 邓通 - A historical figure during the reign of Emperor Wen of Han, known for his close relationship with the emperor and his eventual downfall due to the emperor’s changing favor.
  • 蜀郡南安 - A geographical location in ancient Sichuan, corresponding to modern-day Nanchong or southern Sichuan.
  • 文帝 - Emperor Wen of Han (r. 179–157 BCE), known for his frugal and benevolent rule.
  • 洗沐 - A term for official leave, allowing officials to rest or attend to personal matters.
  • 相人 - A practice of Chinese physiognomy, where one’s face or body is read to predict their fate or fortune.
  • 上 - A common term for 'the emperor' in classical Chinese.

[1008]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 于是赐通蜀严道铜山 - Formal expression indicating a royal grant of land or resources.
  • 尝自铸钱 - Refers to the act of privately minting currency.
  • 邓氏钱布天下 - Metaphorical expression indicating the widespread influence or dominance of Deng's currency.
  • 文帝尝病痈 - Medical term for a serious skin infection, often used to describe a serious illness.
  • 邓通常为上嗽吮之 - Literal translation of a highly sensitive and intimate act, considered inappropriate in modern contexts.
  • 上不乐 - Concise expression of displeasure.
  • 从容问曰 - Indicates a relaxed or offhand manner of speaking.
  • 宜莫若太子 - Formal expression of preference.
  • 太子人间疾 - Here likely means 'in private' or 'secretly.'
  • 上使太子痈 - Character '' is likely '吮' (to suck), making the phrase 'ordered the Crown Prince to suck the boil.'
  • 太子而色难之 - Indicates reluctance or unwillingness.
  • 已而，闻通尝为上之 - Indicates a change in perspective after learning new information.
  • 由是心恨通 - Common expression of developing a grudge.
  • 及文帝崩 - Standard expression of the emperor's death.
  • 景帝立 - Indicates the succession of emperors.
  • 邓通免家居 - Indicates his removal from official duties.
  • 居亡何 - Classical expression for a short period of time.
  • 人有告通盗出徼外铸钱 - '徼外' refers to areas beyond the frontier or borders.
  • 下吏验问颇有 - Indicates the investigation yielded some findings.
  • 遂竟案 - Indicates the legal process was completed.
  • 尽没入之 - Refers to the seizure of property.
  • 通家尚负债数矩万 - '数矩万' is likely a variant or corrupted form of '数百万' (several hundred thousand).
  • 长公主赐邓通，吏辄没入之 - Indicates the confiscation of all property.
  • 一簪不得着身 - Metaphor for complete destitution.
  • 于是长公主乃令假衣食 - Indicates the princess's reluctant assistance.
  • 竟不得名一钱 - Emphasizes his complete financial ruin.
  • 寄死人家 - Euphemism for dying in poverty, without a home of one's own.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邓通 - A historical figure in the Western Han dynasty, known for his close relationship with Emperor Wen and his later downfall due to accusations of coin counterfeiting.
  • 文帝 - The second emperor of the Western Han dynasty, known for his lenient and Confucian policies.
  • 景帝 - The third emperor of the Western Han dynasty, son of Emperor Wen.
  • 长公主 - Likely refers to the Princess Liu Fei, daughter of Emperor Wen, who had a close relationship with Deng Tong.
  • 痈 - A serious skin infection, often associated with illness or weakness in classical Chinese medical texts.
  • 铸钱 - A highly controlled activity in ancient China, typically reserved for the state. Private minting was illegal and punishable by death.
  • 徼外 - Refers to areas outside the Han dynasty's administrative control or frontier regions.
  • 没入 - A legal term for the seizure of property, often used in cases of treason or financial crime.
  • 寄死人家 - A euphemism for dying in poverty, without a home of one's own.

Proper Nouns

  • 邓通 - A historical figure in the Western Han dynasty.
  • 文帝 - The second emperor of the Western Han dynasty.
  • 景帝 - The third emperor of the Western Han dynasty.
  • 长公主 - Likely refers to Princess Liu Fei, daughter of Emperor Wen.
  • 蜀 - A region in present-day Sichuan province.
  • 严道 - A place name, likely a county or region in Sichuan.
  • 铜山 - A place known for copper mining.
  • 太子 - The heir apparent to the Han throne.

[1009]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 愈益親焉 - Indicates a deepening relationship over time.
  • 以好聞 - Because of his good reputation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 韓嫣 - A prominent figure in the court of Emperor Wu, known for his close relationship with the emperor and his eventual downfall.
  • 弓高侯 - A noble title, indicating a high-ranking official or noble.
  • 武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han, one of the most powerful and influential emperors of the Han dynasty.
  • 鄧通 - A famous eunuch who was heavily favored by Emperor Wu, known for his wealth and influence.
  • 巫蛊 - A period of political turmoil in Emperor Wu's reign, characterized by accusations of witchcraft and rebellion.
  • 戾太子 - The rebellious crown prince, who was involved in the political conflict during the witchcraft incident.

Proper Nouns

  • 韓嫣 - A prominent figure in the court of Emperor Wu.
  • 弓高侯 - A noble title.
  • 武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 鄧通 - A famous eunuch who was heavily favored by Emperor Wu.
  • 江都王 - A prince of the Han dynasty.
  • 皇太后 - The empress dowager, the mother of Emperor Wu.
  • 龙雒侯 - A title granted to Han Yan's brother.
  • 大司马 - A high-ranking military title.
  • 车骑将军 - A high-ranking military title.

[1010]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 金丸 - A golden ball, often used as a metaphor for something valuable or precious.
  • 常以金为丸 - Literally means 'often made into golden balls,' indicating a habit of using gold to make balls.
  • 所失者日有十余 - Means 'daily losing over ten,' suggesting a significant number of losses.
  • 若饥寒，逐金丸 - A proverbial saying meaning 'if hungry and cold, chase the golden ball,' implying that people will pursue valuable things even in difficult circumstances.
  • 京师儿童每闻嫣出弹，辄随之，望丸之所落 - Means 'children in the capital always follow whenever Yan shoots, hoping to catch the ball,' indicating the popularity and allure of Yan's activity.
  • 延年坐法腐刑，给事狗监中 - Means 'Yan was punished with castration for breaking the law and was assigned to work in the dog kennel,' describing his punishment and subsequent occupation.
  • 女弟，得幸于上，号李夫人，列外戚传 - Means 'his sister was favored by the emperor, known as Lady Li, and was included in the annals of the imperial family,' highlighting his sister's rise to prominence.
  • 延年善歌，为新变声 - Means 'Yan was good at singing and composed new melodies,' indicating his musical talent.
  • 是时方兴天地诸词 - Means 'at that time, various poetic forms were emerging,' referring to the development of new literary styles.
  • 欲造，令司马相如等作诗颂，延年辄承意弦歌，所造诗为之新声曲 - Means 'the emperor wanted to create new works, so he ordered Sima Xiangru and others to write poems and odes, and Yan would accompany them on the zither, creating new musical pieces,' describing his role in the imperial court.
  • 而李夫人产昌邑王 - Means 'and Lady Li gave birth to Prince of Changyi,' indicating a significant event in the imperial family.
  • 延年由是贵，为协律都尉、佩二千石印缓，而与上卧起，其爱幸埒韩嫣 - Means 'Yan became highly favored, serving as the Chief Musician, wearing a seal of the second rank, and living closely with the emperor, his favor equal to that of Yan,' describing his elevated status.
  • 久之，延年弟季与中人乱，出骄恣 - Means 'after a long time, Yan's younger brother Ji had an affair with a palace eunuch and became arrogant,' indicating a decline in his family's standing.
  • 及李夫人卒后，其爱弛，上遂诛延年兄弟宗族 - Means 'after Lady Li died, his favor waned, and the emperor eventually executed Yan and his brothers and relatives,' describing the consequences of his sister's death.
  • 是后宠臣，大抵外戚之家也 - Means 'after that, favored officials were mostly from the imperial family,' indicating a shift in the composition of the imperial court.
  • 卫青、霍去病皆爱幸，然亦以功能自进 - Means 'Wei Qing and Huo Qubing were also favored, but they rose to power through their abilities,' highlighting the importance of merit in the imperial court.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金丸 - A metaphor for something valuable, often used in literature to symbolize wealth or status.
  • 李延年 - A famous musician and singer during the Han Dynasty, known for his talent and influence in the imperial court.
  • 李夫人 - A concubine of Emperor Wu of Han, known for her beauty and the influence she wielded in the court.
  • 司马相如 - A renowned poet and writer during the Han Dynasty, known for his literary works and contributions to Chinese literature.
  • 卫青 - A general and military leader during the Han Dynasty, known for his victories against the Xiongnu.
  • 霍去病 - A general and military leader during the Han Dynasty, known for his military prowess and contributions to the expansion of the Han Empire.
  • 外戚 - Refers to relatives of the emperor by marriage, often holding significant power and influence in the imperial court.

Proper Nouns

  • 韩嫣 - A courtier and favorite of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 李延年 - A musician and singer during the Han Dynasty.
  • 李夫人 - A concubine of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 司马相如 - A renowned poet and writer during the Han Dynasty.
  • 卫青 - A general and military leader during the Han Dynasty.
  • 霍去病 - A general and military leader during the Han Dynasty.
  • 外戚 - Relatives of the emperor by marriage.
  • 昌邑王 - A title given to the son of Lady Li.
  • 协律都尉 - A high-ranking official responsible for music and ceremonies in the imperial court.
  • 二千石 - A rank in the Han Dynasty bureaucracy.
  • 中人 - Palace eunuchs.
